dependency_controls.circuit_breaker: 3 consecutive failures → open for 60s; half-open with one probe
dependency_controls.notes: High-criticality boundary; budget retries tightly to preserve the caller's SLA. Idempotent operation: retry is safe without explicit idempotency key.

dependency_controls.circuit_breaker: 3 consecutive failures → open for 60s; half-open with one probe
dependency_controls.notes: High-criticality boundary; budget retries tightly to preserve the caller's SLA.

dependency_controls.circuit_breaker: 3 consecutive failures → open for 60s; half-open with one probe

dependency_controls.circuit_breaker: 10% error rate over 1 min → open for 60s
dependency_controls.notes: External dependency; classify errors and skip retry on 4xx; idempotency key mandatory for any mutation. Stated SLA unknown; treat as unreliable.

dependency_controls.circuit_breaker: 10% error rate over 1 min → open for 60s
dependency_controls.notes: External dependency; classify errors and skip retry on 4xx; idempotency key mandatory for any mutation.

bulkhead_notes.notes: Single-replica high-criticality service — replicate to at least 2 instances.
Worker — set a maximum concurrent message count per replica; reject overflow into DLQ.
Worker — set a maximum concurrent message count per replica; reject overflow into DLQ.

load_leveling_notes.notes: Front the worker with a queue (Service Bus or Storage Queue). Set max-delivery-count and DLQ; scale on queue depth.
Front the worker with a queue (Service Bus or Storage Queue). Set max-delivery-count and DLQ; scale on queue depth.

fallbacks.detail: On open breaker, return 503 to caller; surface in dashboard; do not synthesise data.
On open breaker, serve cached/default; surface degraded status in response payload.
On open breaker, return 503 to caller; surface in dashboard; do not synthesise data.
On open breaker, return 503 to caller; surface in dashboard; do not synthesise data.
Cache the last successful response; serve cached on breaker open; queue mutations for replay.
Cache the last successful response; serve cached on breaker open; queue mutations for replay.

next_steps: Implement the dependency controls above using a resilience library (Polly, gobreaker, or platform mesh).
Verify idempotency keys are propagated on every retryable mutation in code review.
Wire the detection signals into Application Insights with dashboards and alerts.
Run a chaos test that opens a circuit breaker in staging and confirms the fallback behaves as designed.
Document the runbook for the most likely incident shape (one downstream becomes slow).
summary: Resilience plan for ecommerce-order-platform: 6 dependency control(s), 3 bulkhead note(s), 2 load-leveling note(s), 6 fallback(s); coverage 100.
